Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Marine Engineering And Supply Company
Off Calendar Feb-22-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Marine Engineering and Supply Company.
Dept. 514. Judge: Cynthia Ming-Mei Lee. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703.

Michael Espinosa, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs Lynn Brazel, as Successor-in-Interest to and as Wrongful Death Heir of Roger Henderson, Deceased; and Heather Howard, Summer Heredia, Brandon Henderson, as Wrongful Death Heirs of Roger Henderson, Deceased.
No appearance by defaulted defendant Marine Engineering and Supply Company.
Complaint was filed on February 25, 2011.
Default was entered against defendant on April 23, 2012.
Statement of Damages was filed and served on April 23, 2012.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was filed on January 8, 2018.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiffs Lynn Brazel, as Successor-in-Interest to and as Wrongful Death Heir of Roger Henderson, Deceased; and Heather Howard, Summer Heredia, Brandon Henderson, as Wrongful Death Heirs of Roger Henderson, Deceased, and against the Defaulted Defendant Marin Engineering and Supply Company, as follows:

Economic Damages: $75,543.91.
Non-Economic Damages - $2,000,000.00.
Total - $2,075,543,91.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kevin T. Barger of DEHAY ELLISTON LLP (510-285-0747), appearing for defendant / moving party Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Roger Henderson

RULING:
DEFENDANT KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiff's responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Miles Lendway of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13684 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Associated Insulation Of California
Off Calendar Apr-30-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Donald Sullivan, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, CSR: Sonia Wong, #11288.

Oren Noah, Esq., of Brayton and Purcell appearing for Plaintiff, Roger Henderson.
Order Granting waiver of Witness Requirement of Hearing on Application for Default Prove-up, on file.
No appearance by defendant(s) ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA.

The court stated on the record:
In this personal injury asbestos case, the law allows joint and several liability for the economic damages sought.
However, for the non-economic damages sought, the law allows only several liability, i.e., each party or person is responsible for its, his or her own specific percentage of fault.
In this Request to a Enter Default Judgment, there is absolutely no evidence demonstrating the particular percentage of fault of this defendant in default.
The individual statement of damages is not helpful. It does not relate to any evidence of any amount of total non-economic damages nor any specific percentage of fault attributed to the defaulting defendant. The Statement of Damages merely acts as a ceiling on the amount of non-economic damages that the court could award in this setting and little more.
Therefore, under CCP section 579, in my discretion, I do not find it to be proper to enter a several Default Judgment against this defaulting defendant. The motion or Request for several Default Judgment is denied without prejudice and the court defers it to be heard at a later time by the trial court judge. It is my understanding that this matter is currently set for trial on June 10, 2013.
